content.body: A MATRIX is a rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns. The ORDER (size) of a matrix is given as rows × columns (m × n).

Matrix A = [a b / c d] is a 2×2 matrix (2 rows, 2 columns)
Element notation: aᵢⱼ = element in row i, column j
ADDITION/SUBTRACTION: Only possible when matrices have the SAME order. Add or subtract corresponding elements.
[a b / c d] + [e f / g h] = [a+e  b+f / c+g  d+h]
SCALAR MULTIPLICATION: Multiply every element in the matrix by the scalar (number).

questions.question: The order of a matrix with 3 rows and 2 columns is:
questions.explanation: Order = rows × columns. 3 rows and 2 columns = 3×2 matrix.

questions.question: Matrix addition is only possible if matrices have:
options: The same order (same rows AND columns)
questions.answer: The same order (same rows AND columns)
questions.explanation: You can only add corresponding elements, so matrices must be exactly the same size.

questions.question: A 2×3 matrix has:
questions.explanation: m×n = rows × columns. 2×3 = 2 rows, 3 columns, 6 elements total.

questions.question: The zero matrix (null matrix) has all elements equal to:
questions.explanation: The zero/null matrix has every element = 0. It is the identity element for matrix addition (A + 0 = A).

questions.question: Matrix A is 2×3 and matrix B is 3×2. Can you add A + B?
options: Yes only if elements are equal
questions.explanation: A is 2×3, B is 3×2. Different dimensions — matrix addition requires identical orders.

questions.question: In matrix A, element a₂₁ refers to element in:
questions.explanation: aᵢⱼ: i = row number, j = column number. a₂₁ = row 2, column 1.

questions.question: A square matrix has:
options: Equal number of rows and columns
questions.answer: Equal number of rows and columns
questions.explanation: Square matrix: n×n. Examples: 2×2, 3×3. Most important operations (determinant, inverse) require square matrices.

questions.question: The transpose of matrix A (written Aᵀ) is formed by:
questions.explanation: Transpose: row i becomes column i. A 2×3 matrix has a 3×2 transpose.

questions.question: A row matrix (row vector) has:
questions.explanation: Row matrix (1×n): one row, many columns, e.g. [3 1 4 1 5].

questions.question: If A = [x / y] and B = [3 / 4] and A + B = [7 / 6], find x and y:
A 1×1 matrix [k] is essentially the same as:
questions.explanation: A 1×1 matrix contains one number — it behaves exactly like a scalar.

questions.question: Matrix algebra is used in real life for:
options: Computer graphics, solving simultaneous equations, data science
questions.answer: Computer graphics, solving simultaneous equations, data science
questions.explanation: Matrices are everywhere: transformations in 3D graphics, solving systems of equations, machine learning, economics.

questions.question: A + B = B + A for matrices. This is the ___ property.
questions.explanation: Matrix addition is commutative: A+B = B+A (order doesn't matter). Note: matrix MULTIPLICATION is NOT commutative.

questions.question: To find 3A − 2B where A=[2 1/0 3] and B=[1 2/1 0], element at row 1 col 1 is:
